hints.junior: outline: none on :focus removes keyboard navigation visibility for all users. It's one of the most common accessibility mistakes in real codebases.
hints.mid: :focus-visible is the correct replacement: it shows the ring for keyboard users and hides it for mouse users. Browser support is now universal.
hints.staff: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.11 is now AA, so focus indicator requirements are no longer optional. Check your design system's focus ring against the contrast and size criteria.

hints.junior: Tab through the demo in broken mode: can you tell where you are? Now try fixed mode. The ring shows exactly where keyboard focus is.
hints.mid: Notice: in fixed mode, clicking a button doesn't show the ring. Tabbing to it does. That's :focus-visible working correctly.
hints.staff: The fixed ring uses outline, not box-shadow. outline renders in Windows High Contrast mode; box-shadow doesn't. Always use outline for focus indicators.

hints.junior: Think of :focus-visible as a bouncer who only checks IDs for people who walked in, not the ones who teleported: click with a mouse and there's no ring, tab with a keyboard and the browser shows one automatically.
hints.mid: The spec's heuristic — did the element receive focus via keyboard, or is it a type that usually benefits from a visible ring — is what MDN documents under :focus-visible. You don't need to reverse-engineer it, just don't override it for mouse clicks.
hints.staff: The heuristic keeps a 'recently used keyboard' flag, so tabbing into a field then clicking a button can still show the ring on the click. That's not a bug to suppress; the spec assumes a keyboard user benefits from it even then.

hints.junior: Never write *:focus { outline: none } alone. Pair every suppression with a :focus-visible rule, or keyboard users lose their ring entirely.
hints.mid: Put the ring on outline, not box-shadow: outline survives Windows High Contrast Mode and doesn't affect layout. box-shadow silently vanishes for the users who need it most.
hints.staff: Treat --focus-ring-color as a token, not a per-component color — and verify it renders, don't just declare it. A static analyzer can't see a rendered focus ring, so add a Playwright pass that tabs through nav in both light and dark mode and asserts a visible outline is actually painted.

anecdotes.junior: My first accessibility audit found outline: none on every interactive element, added by a designer who found the default ring ugly. :focus-visible was the clean fix.
anecdotes.mid: The CSS reset * { outline: none } is still in many starter templates and tutorial codebases. Always replace it with a :focus-visible rule.
anecdotes.staff: I've seen teams pass WCAG audits by adding outline: 2px solid transparent for keyboard users (technically visible in high contrast mode but invisible elsewhere). Don't do this.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're fixing one of the most common accessibility mistakes: removing the focus ring and making keyboard navigation invisible. The fix is one CSS rule.
sections.mid: This case covers :focus-visible: the CSS pseudo-class that shows focus rings for keyboard users only, giving you design control without the accessibility cost.
sections.staff: We're covering WCAG 2.2 focus appearance requirements, the :focus-visible browser heuristic, and design system token patterns for focus indicators.

sections.junior: The concept chapter is the principle: read it before the demo so the toggle makes sense.
sections.mid: Connect the principle to your component API: what prop or CSS change encodes the fix?
sections.staff: Map the pattern to observability: what would you measure in RUM or lab to prove the fix?

sections.junior: Tab through the demo in broken mode: can you tell where you are? Now try fixed mode. The ring shows exactly where keyboard focus is.
sections.mid: Notice: in fixed mode, clicking a button doesn't show the ring. Tabbing to it does. That's :focus-visible working correctly.
sections.staff: The fixed ring uses outline, not box-shadow. outline renders in Windows High Contrast mode; box-shadow doesn't. Always use outline for focus indicators.

sections.junior: This section covers takeaway for focus-visible-not-outline-none.
